tests.name: complete local quality-gate component set
tests.summary: Dependency installation, Go formatting, module verification, vet, unit tests, race tests, reproducible linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 builds, MCP and worker smokes, 55 Python tests, and repository validation passed. The kernel smoke was rerun outside the chat sandbox because that sandbox denies loopback bind; daemon readiness, CLI create/get, and graceful shutdown then passed.

tests.name: retrieval contract and adversarial unit suite
tests.summary: Contracts, deterministic cards, sparse encoding, rank fusion, canonical resolution, stale and cross-scope rejection, Qdrant request validation, Bedrock vector validation, evaluation scoring, private capture validation, bounded deadlines, and telemetry behavior passed under unit and race execution.

tests.name: public exact-basis CI
tests.summary: Public GitHub Actions run 29726147010 on basis commit a9a971cff16ee50ab4fafca3a475f117a47f438d passed repository validation, PostgreSQL durability, the pinned live-Qdrant lifecycle, and the clean-host observability rehearsal.

tests.name: PostgreSQL retrieval integration suite
tests.summary: The public CI PostgreSQL durability gate covered migration 009, projector registration and fan-out, claims and fencing, contiguous checkpoints, replay, generation activation, point receipts, canonical symbol/test/memory/incident resolution, rollback refusal after canonical history, and cross-store alias-mutation serialization with a bounded guard context.

tests.name: historical rollback compatibility after migration 009
tests.summary: Sprint 08-to-07, Sprint 07-to-06, and Sprint 05-to-04 binary compatibility rehearsals dynamically rolled a disposable PostgreSQL 18.4 schema from migration 009 to each historical target and passed.

tests.name: live Qdrant lifecycle
tests.summary: Public CI ran the lifecycle against digest-pinned Qdrant v1.18.2 through the required guard interface: physical collection creation, first alias creation, atomic delete-and-create replacement, blue-green cutover, readback, guarded rollback, dense and sparse query paths, deletion, and cleanup all passed. PostgreSQL serialization is proven separately by the PostgreSQL integration suite; no composed test is claimed.
